novel_features.name: Buyer dossier
novel_features.description: Full profile for one contracting authority: spending cadence, top CPVs, top procedures, active vs awarded vs cancelled counts over a window.
novel_features.rationale: TenderNed exposes no aggregation endpoint — this is a pure SQLite GROUP BY join across notices cached locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user wants the full procurement picture of one buyer in one call instead of paginating through their notice list.
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

novel_features.name: Sub-threshold lead finder
novel_features.description: Filter notices to national-only AND below an estimated-value cap AND inside CPV stripes — the long-tail tenders that EU TED never sees.
novel_features.rationale: Sub-EU-threshold notices are TenderNed-only. The local store plus multi-CPV intersection turns a manual page-walk into one query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when serving a Dutch SME or municipality-focused supplier whose target contracts are below EU thresholds.
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

novel_features.name: Closing-deadline view
novel_features.description: Lists notices closing within N days, grouped by day, with countdown plus buyer plus procedure filters.
novel_features.rationale: Local indexed query on sluitingsDatum joined with arbitrary filters — no API surface composes deadline plus CPV plus buyer plus value cap in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when triaging which open tenders need a response this week. Mirrors eu-tenders deadline.
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

novel_features.name: Watch saved query
novel_features.description: Persist a filter set; watch run returns only NEW notices since the cursor's last advance.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite stores both the filter JSON and the last-seen publicatieDatum; the cursor makes incremental delivery cheap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user wants a recurring filter without an external alerting system.

novel_features.name: CPV-drift over time
novel_features.description: Year-by-year top-CPV table for one buyer or NUTS region; shows whether a buyer's purchasing mix is shifting.
novel_features.rationale: Historical aggregation across multiple years requires a local snapshot; the live API has no historical aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when answering 'is this buyer's spending mix changing?' for a market-trend analysis. Mirrors eu-tenders cpv-drift.

novel_features.name: TED cross-reference
novel_features.description: Extracts the canonical TED publication number from the eForms XML, prints it plus the TED URL.
novel_features.rationale: The TED number is buried in the eForms XML, not in the JSON list response; only this CLI surfaces it on demand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants to pivot from a TenderNed notice to the EU-wide TED record (e.g., for use with the sibling eu-tenders CLI).

novel_features.name: Tender-thread reconcile
novel_features.description: Walks PIN to CN to CAN to Modification chains for one buyer; flags orphan PINs, orphan CNs, and unresolved modifications.
novel_features.rationale: eForms predecessor links are TenderNed-specific data not exposed in the JSON list response; lifecycle reconciliation is impossible without parsing every XML.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a compliance officer is auditing whether announced tenders were actually awarded or cancelled.

novel_features.name: Buyer top-N aggregation
novel_features.description: Top contracting authorities by tender count for a CPV plus NUTS plus date slice.
novel_features.rationale: Pure SQLite GROUP BY aggregation; TenderNed exposes no aggregation endpoint and the API limits per-call results.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'which buyers are most active in this slice?'
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

novel_features.name: Document grep
novel_features.description: Streams documents from matching notices, runs a regex against PDF and Word text, prints pub_id, doc_id, line hits.
novel_features.rationale: Bulk grep across bestek and PvE document bodies is unique to a local CLI; the web UI lets you download one at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants to find every notice mentioning a clause, vendor, or technical requirement across many bestek documents.

novel_features.name: Weekly notice velocity
novel_features.description: Weekly publication-count trend over the local snapshot; spot heating or cooling markets per CPV or buyer.
novel_features.rationale: Time-series rollup over the local notices table; impossible without a snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when sizing market momentum for one CPV or buyer. Mirrors eu-tenders velocity.

novel_features.name: Buyer concentration (HHI)
novel_features.description: Herfindahl-Hirschman index of award concentration for a CPV plus NUTS slice; tells you whether a market is dominated by one buyer or fragmented.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite aggregation; the API has no aggregation surface.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when assessing whether to bid in a market dominated by a few incumbents. Mirrors eu-tenders concentration.
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

novel_features.name: Deadline-heat calendar
novel_features.description: Ranked calendar of expiring notices weighted by urgency times estimated value; daily prioritized view of what needs attention.
novel_features.rationale: Combines local sluitingsDatum index with value-band weighting; no API endpoint produces this ranking.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when sequencing bid effort across many open deadlines. Mirrors eu-tenders deadline-heat.
novel_features.group: Local aggregations that beat the API

narrative.headline: Every Dutch public tender, with the sub-threshold long tail that EU TED never sees, in a local-first CLI you can pipe.
narrative.value_prop: TenderNed is the central Dutch national procurement platform; all 143,000+ live notices are CC-0 licensed and freely accessible. This CLI puts every publication, every contracting authority, and every attached bestek into a local SQLite store you can grep, join, and aggregate offline. The novel commands — buyer dossier, sub-threshold leads, closing-deadline view, CPV drift over time, tender-thread reconcile — are workflows the official web UI cannot do.
narrative.auth_narrative: Most of TenderNed (search, list, document download, RSS) is unauthenticated and works out of the box. Only the full eForms XML endpoint requires a free username and password — request via functioneelbeheer@tenderned.nl, then export TENDERNED_USERNAME and TENDERNED_PASSWORD.

quickstart.comment: List the latest tender notices as JSON
Populate the local SQLite store so offline queries and novel commands work
Compute a full procurement profile for one contracting authority
Find sub-threshold construction tenders that never reach EU TED
Triage what's closing in the next two weeks

troubleshoots.fix: The eForms XML endpoint needs Basic auth. Set TENDERNED_USERNAME and TENDERNED_PASSWORD; request credentials from functioneelbeheer@tenderned.nl if you don't have them.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for valid-looking CPV filter
troubleshoots.fix: TenderNed expects the full 8-digit-plus-check-digit CPV form (e.g. `45000000-7`, not `45000000`). Pass it with the dash and check digit.

troubleshoots.symptom: Novel commands (dossier, leads, drift) return no data
troubleshoots.fix: Run `tenderned-pp-cli sync --since <date>` first — these commands query the local SQLite store, not the live API.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick this CLI when the user is working in the Dutch procurement market and needs sub-threshold coverage, document-corpus search, per-buyer analytics, or anything that requires holding more than one publication in working memory at once. For above-threshold EU-wide queries (TED publication numbers, cross-country buyer dossiers), pair it with the eu-tenders CLI.

recipes.title: Daily new-opportunity sweep
recipes.explanation: Narrow the fat notice payload to just the fields a triage agent needs.

recipes.title: Cross-reference a notice to EU TED
recipes.explanation: Extract the canonical TED publication number so you can pivot to the eu-tenders CLI for EU-wide analysis.

recipes.title: Find every bestek mentioning a clause
recipes.explanation: Bulk regex across attached tender documents; unique to a local CLI.

recipes.title: Save a query and return only new hits
recipes.explanation: Watch returns only NEW notices since the cursor's last advance. Create the watch first with 'watch add civils <filter-json>'.

recipes.title: Buyer purchasing-mix drift
recipes.explanation: Year-bucket CPV-2-digit distribution for one buyer — answers 'is their spending mix shifting?'
